name: R5AddressElementMapToR4
title: Cross-version mapping for FHIR R5 Address to FHIR R4 Address
description: This ConceptMap represents cross-version mappings for elements from a FHIR R5 Address to FHIR R4.

target.comment:
Element `Address.use` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `Address.use` as `Equivalent`.
Applications can assume that an address is current unless it explicitly says that it is temporary or old.

Element `Address.use` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `Address.use` as `Equivalent`.
Applications can assume that an address is current unless it explicitly says that it is temporary or old.

Element `Address.type` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `Address.type` as `Equivalent`.
The definition of Address states that "address is intended to describe postal addresses, not physical locations". However, many applications track whether an address has a dual purpose of being a location that can be visited as well as being a valid delivery destination, and Postal addresses are often used as proxies for physical locations (also see the [Location](https://hl7.org/fhir/l.html#) resource).

Element `Address.type` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `Address.type` as `Equivalent`.
The definition of Address states that "address is intended to describe postal addresses, not physical locations". However, many applications track whether an address has a dual purpose of being a location that can be visited as well as being a valid delivery destination, and Postal addresses are often used as proxies for physical locations (also see the [Location](https://hl7.org/fhir/l.html#) resource).

Element `Address.text` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `Address.text` as `Equivalent`.
Can provide both a text representation and parts. Applications updating an address SHALL ensure that  when both text and parts are present,  no content is included in the text that isn't found in a part.

Element `Address.text` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `Address.text` as `Equivalent`.
Can provide both a text representation and parts. Applications updating an address SHALL ensure that  when both text and parts are present,  no content is included in the text that isn't found in a part.
Element `Address.line` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `Address.line` as `Equivalent`.
Element `Address.line` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `Address.line` as `Equivalent`.
Element `Address.city` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `Address.city` as `Equivalent`.
Element `Address.city` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `Address.city` as `Equivalent`.

Element `Address.district` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `Address.district` as `Equivalent`.
District is sometimes known as county, but in some regions 'county' is used in place of city (municipality), so county name should be conveyed in city instead.

Element `Address.district` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `Address.district` as `Equivalent`.
District is sometimes known as county, but in some regions 'county' is used in place of city (municipality), so county name should be conveyed in city instead.
Element `Address.state` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `Address.state` as `Equivalent`.
Element `Address.state` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `Address.state` as `Equivalent`.
Element `Address.postalCode` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `Address.postalCode` as `Equivalent`.
Element `Address.postalCode` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `Address.postalCode` as `Equivalent`.

Element `Address.country` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `Address.country` as `Equivalent`.
ISO 3166 2- or 3- letter codes MAY be used in place of a human readable country name.

Element `Address.country` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `Address.country` as `Equivalent`.
ISO 3166 2- or 3- letter codes MAY be used in place of a human readable country name.
Element `Address.period` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `Address.period` as `Equivalent`.
Element `Address.period` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `Address.period` as `Equivalent`.
